_comment: NAVADA MolecularAtlas catalogue. Each entry is a real 3D structure loaded live from the Protein Data Bank (pdb) or PubChem (cid) by 3Dmol.js — no local model files. 'sites' are highlightable regions/residues. Generated/extended by the Claude Science 'neuroatlas-asset' skill.

structures.name: SARS-CoV-2 Spike Protein
structures.summary: The spike (S) glycoprotein that studs the surface of the SARS-CoV-2 virus and lets it enter human cells. This is the prefusion trimer — three copies clasped together. The tip of one chain is flipped 'up', exposing the receptor-binding domain that grabs the human ACE2 receptor. It is the part most vaccines train your immune system to recognise.

sites.name: Receptor-binding domain (RBD)
sites.function: The hook that latches onto the human ACE2 receptor to start infection. It is the main target of neutralising antibodies and most vaccines.

sites.name: N-terminal domain (NTD)
sites.function: The leading edge of the spike. It helps the virus attach and is a hotspot where mutations change how the immune system sees the virus.

structures.name: Main Protease + Inhibitor (a cure in action)
structures.summary: The SARS-CoV-2 main protease (Mpro), the molecular scissors the virus needs to mature and replicate. Here a drug-like inhibitor (N3) sits jammed inside the active site, blocking the scissors so the virus cannot copy itself. This is exactly the mechanism antiviral pills like Paxlovid use — and the template for designing one.

sites.name: Bound inhibitor (N3)
sites.function: The drug molecule wedged into the enzyme's mouth. By filling the active site it stops the protease from cutting viral proteins — halting replication.

sites.name: Catalytic Cys145
sites.function: Half of the catalytic dyad — the reactive cysteine that does the cutting. The inhibitor forms a bond right here to disable it.

sites.name: Catalytic His41
sites.function: The other half of the catalytic dyad. It primes the cysteine; block these two residues and the enzyme is dead.

structures.name: Aspirin (small-molecule drug)
structures.summary: Acetylsalicylic acid — aspirin — fetched straight from PubChem as a 3D conformer. A tiny molecule compared with a protein, it works by permanently acetylating the COX enzymes, dialling down the chemicals that cause pain, fever and clotting. A clean example of the 'cure' scale the atlas can render alongside its targets.
